title: Lab: Deliver an Accessible Transit-Visit Guide
summary: Lab: Deliver an Accessible Transit-Visit Guide uses an original transit visit guide handoff scenario to retrieve earlier HTML skills, add a bounded artifact layer, and verify transfer with source and browser evidence.
objectives: Retrieve html text headings lists, html links urls, html images alt without step-by-step imitation.
Produce and verify a real transit visit guide handoff artifact or evidence report.
Preserve earlier valid requirements while diagnosing changed content and constraints.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Build the public information artifact here. -->

steps.title: Triage the transit visit guide handoff · lab
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which observation identifies the highest-value source boundary to test first? Work context: transit visit guide handoff.
steps.why: Lab: Deliver an Accessible Transit-Visit Guide requires this decision because each decision must preserve document meaning, user control, portability, and inspectable evidence. This is checkpoint 1 in the transit visit guide handoff.

content.title: transit visit guide handoff · evidence decision
content.text: Commit before feedback, then connect the selected claim to the transit visit guide handoff evidence.

hints: Lab: Deliver an Accessible Transit-Visit Guide: first isolate the evidence required by “Triage the transit visit guide handoff.”
Lab: Deliver an Accessible Transit-Visit Guide: compare the current artifact with the named predict goal for step 1.
Lab: Deliver an Accessible Transit-Visit Guide: use the smallest defensible change or claim that satisfies “Triage the transit visit guide handoff.”

options.text: Transit visit guide handoff case 1: a sequence of br elements communicates the same list relationship as ol or ul.
Transit visit guide handoff case 1: select text elements for the relationship they communicate, then use CSS later for visual scale.
Transit visit guide handoff case 1: every sentence should be a heading because headings receive stronger default styling.

options.text: Transit visit guide handoff case 3: heading rank is a font-size control, so smaller-looking sections should always use h6.
Transit visit guide handoff case 3: select text elements for the relationship they communicate, then use CSS later for visual scale.
Transit visit guide handoff case 3: every sentence should be a heading because headings receive stronger default styling.
